skill.name: agile-design-doc
skill.description: Creates concise agile design documents by reviewing project context, identifying reusable components, and outlining MVP-focused implementation plans.
skill.summary: Generate focused MVP design documents for agile software teams.
skill.author: AriesYB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, reconnaissance, weak cryptography, and entropy indicators, but review found markdown examples, Mermaid diagrams, front matter, and Chinese documentation text. The only confirmed risk is expected filesystem reading of project configuration, source structure, README, and docs to create a design document. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, secret harvesting, executable payload, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Expected Project File Inspection
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read project configuration files, directory structure, README files, documentation, and existing code before writing the design document. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but users should run it only in projects where that inspection is allowed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly request reading local project files for technical context. The behavior is scoped to documentation generation and has no network or secret exfiltration path.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections point to markdown code fences, Mermaid diagrams, Python examples, JSON examples, and procedural text. I found no command execution instruction, shell invocation, subprocess call, or dynamic execution payload in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every reviewed location is documentation syntax or example content. The files contain no executable script body and no instruction to run arbitrary commands.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto, Reconnaissance, and Entropy Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and entropy alerts occur in front matter and natural-language documentation, not cryptographic code or obfuscated payloads. The reconnaissance alerts map to Mermaid or documentation content, not system inventory commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are front matter, markdown diagram fences, or template content. There is no weak algorithm implementation, binary blob, encrypted data, or host reconnaissance command.

content.value_statement: Teams often need design documents that are useful without becoming large specifications. This skill reviews project context and produces concise MVP-focused plans for implementation.
actual_capabilities: Reviews project configuration files to infer the technology stack.
Identifies reusable components from source folders and documentation.
Creates concise design goals, feature lists, and implementation plans.
Uses Mermaid diagrams to describe important interaction flows.
Documents data models, API changes, technical risks, and MVP boundaries.
Checks the final document for focus, accuracy, and unnecessary scope.
limitations: It does not implement the feature or modify production code.
It needs access to relevant project files for accurate recommendations.
It may ask clarifying questions when business context is missing.
It provides planning guidance, not a formal architecture review.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Create a focused design document before a sprint starts.

use_cases.title: Align on Module Boundaries
use_cases.description: Describe responsibilities, interfaces, and reusable components for a new module.

use_cases.title: Prepare Implementation Handoff
use_cases.description: Turn a product request into a developer-ready plan with diagrams and key methods.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a small feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agile-design-doc skill to create a concise design document for this feature: [describe feature]. Focus on MVP scope.

prompt_templates.title: Design From Existing Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt when project files are available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agile-design-doc skill. Review the project stack and reusable components, then design [feature]. Include flow, implementation plan, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: API and Data Model Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agile-design-doc skill to design [backend change]. Include API endpoints, data model changes, integration points, and MVP tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Team Design Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agile-design-doc skill to create a design document for [initiative]. Compare options, identify reusable components, map dependencies, and separate MVP from later iterations.

output_examples.input: Design a user invitation flow for an existing SaaS product.
output_examples.output: A concise design document with goals, feature list, invitation sequence flow, API summary, data model changes, and implementation notes.

output_examples.input: Plan a notification preferences module for a web application.
output_examples.output: A developer-facing plan that identifies current stack assumptions, reusable settings components, data fields, service methods, and MVP boundaries.

output_examples.input: Create a design for adding order export to an admin system.
output_examples.output: A focused proposal with interaction flow, export endpoint design, background job considerations, error handling, and follow-up items.

best_practices: Provide the feature goal, business context, and known constraints before running the skill.
Allow access only to project files that are needed for the design task.
Review assumptions, optional items, and Mermaid diagrams before implementation starts.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for implementation or test creation.
Do not ask for broad architecture redesign when an MVP plan is enough.
Do not run it on confidential repositories without approval to inspect files.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates concise technical design documents for agile software development work.

faq.question: Does it change code?
faq.answer: No. It produces planning documentation and example method outlines only.

faq.question: Why does it read project files?
faq.answer: It reads configuration, source structure, and docs to match the existing technology stack.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Mermaid sequence diagrams and flow diagrams when they clarify system behavior.

faq.question: Is it suitable for large architecture decisions?
faq.answer: It can support discussion, but it is designed for focused MVP-oriented feature planning.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
